reasoning: Being the top Spotify artist for a given year requires massive accumulated daily streams across an artist's entire catalog, typically heavily favoring prolific artists with huge back catalogs like Taylor Swift, Bad Bunny, and Drake. In late 2024 and early 2025, Swift's daily run rate was nearly double that of Billie Eilish (~60M vs ~35-40M streams per day). Furthermore, sources tracking 2025 performance explicitly flag Taylor Swift and The Weeknd as the top contenders for the 2025 title. As of July 2025, Billie Eilish ranked only 9th in all-time streams with 49 billion, compared to Taylor Swift at over 105 billion, highlighting a massive gap in catalog consumption. While Billie Eilish consistently ranks incredibly high in 'monthly listeners' (a metric that counts unique users rather than total streams), 'most streamed artist' counts total plays, heavily favoring the aforementioned giants. Because of this structural gap in daily baseline streams and a lack of evidence suggesting Eilish is dominating total play counts in 2025, the probability of her being the #1 most streamed artist is exceptionally remote.
